source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed., 2021) — Chris McChesney, Sean Covey, Jim Huling, Scott Thele, Beverly Walker
_comment: Stage 4 adversarial pressure test. ≥3 should_trigger (EN/JP/zh-TW), ≥2 should_not_trigger lures (single-team-leader / personal-misfire / member-misfire), ≥1 edge_case (cascade depth).

test_cases.prompt: How do I translate the org Primary WIG to my 5 sub-teams? Each direct-report manager needs their own Team WIG and I need to make sure they ladder up.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade; produce cascade map (Primary WIG → 2-3 Key Battles → N Team WIGs) applying Targets-not-Plans / Battles-to-win-the-war / Veto-don't-Dictate / one-WIG-per-individual; run ladder-up + veto tests.
test_cases.notes: positive scenario EN — canonical 'translate the org WIG to my team' + 'each manager needs a WIG' phrasing in description, 5 sub-teams in 3-7 band.

test_cases.prompt: 上から降りてきた Primary WIG を、配下の 4 チームに翻訳したい。各マネージャーの Team WIG をどう決めればいい？
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade; run the Ch 7 four-rule cascade in Japanese, output cascade map + ladder-up/veto tests.
test_cases.notes: positive scenario JP — canonical 「上から降りた WIG をチーム単位に落とす」「直属マネージャー毎の Team WIG をどう決める」 phrasing.

test_cases.prompt: 上面定的 Primary WIG 我要怎麼分到我帶的 6 個 team？每個主管要有自己的 Team WIG，怎麼選？
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade; run cascade in Traditional Chinese, output cascade map + ladder-up/veto tests.
test_cases.notes: positive scenario zh-TW — canonical 「上面定的 WIG 我要怎麼分到我帶的 team」「每位主管要有自己的 Team WIG，怎麼選？」 phrasing.

test_cases.prompt: I lead a single 8-person team (no sub-teams). Help me pick our team's Primary WIG.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. Single-team leader, no cascade — should route to 4dx-d1-team-primary-wig-selection.
test_cases.notes: lure: single-team-leader — selection-not-cascade; description explicitly excludes 'leader runs only one team'.

test_cases.prompt: Help me define my personal WIG. I want to translate my career goal into quarterly milestones.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. Solo goal — 'translate' + 'milestones' surface match but no org/team context. Should route to 4dx-d1-personal-wig-defining.
test_cases.notes: lure: personal-misfire — 'translate' verb is shared with cascade but scope is individual.

test_cases.prompt: My manager just shared our Team WIG: 'cut response time from 6h to 2h by Q3'. Can you explain what cadence I should expect for the WIG sessions?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate this skill. Team-member comprehension of an already-cascaded Team WIG — should route to a member-comprehension skill.
test_cases.notes: lure: member-misfire — Team WIG mentioned but role is member, cascade already complete, task is comprehension.

test_cases.prompt: I run a division with 4 cascade layers: division → group (3) → team (2-3 each) → squad (2 each). How do I cascade the division Primary WIG all the way down?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade AND advise: book caps cascade depth — re-run this skill once per layer (division→group, then group→team, then team→squad). Veto test must run at every layer. Do not flatten 4 layers into one cascade map.
test_cases.notes: boundary: description explicitly says 'cascade depth >2 layers (rerun this skill per layer)'. Edge tests whether skill catches the depth cap and prescribes the re-run pattern instead of attempting one mega-cascade.

pass_rate_breakdown: 8/8 — all 3 should_trigger PASS (direct match to EN/JP/ZH phrasings in description, sub-team counts in 3-7 band), all 3 should_not_trigger PASS (description explicitly excludes single-team-leader → primary-wig-selection, solo → personal-wig-defining, and member-comprehension is excluded by 'leader-of-leaders' framing), edge-01 (4-layer cascade) PASS — description directly says 'cascade depth >2 layers (rerun this skill per layer)'.
ship_threshold_note: 100% to ship cleanly. ≥80% allowed only with explicit failure analysis. <80% must redo Stage 2 A2/E/B. should_not_trigger has zero tolerance.
